subject: computer-science
description: Inventors & Their Inventions MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Who developed ARPANET?
mcqs.explanation: ARPANET was developed by DARPA (Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency) — the predecessor of the modern Internet.

mcqs.q: Who is the developer of Computer Graphics?
mcqs.explanation: William Fetter is credited as the developer of Computer Graphics — he coined the term 'computer graphics' in 1960.

mcqs.q: Who developed ENIAC?
mcqs.explanation: ENIAC was developed by John Mauchly and J. Presper Eckert.

mcqs.q: Who developed UNIVAC?
mcqs.explanation: UNIVAC — the first commercial computer — was developed by John Mauchly and J. Presper Eckert.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of the C language?
mcqs.explanation: Dennis Ritchie is the Father of the C programming language — he developed it at AT&T in 1972.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of the C++ language?
mcqs.explanation: Bjarne Stroustrup is the Father of C++ — he developed it at AT&T in the mid-1980s.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Java?
mcqs.explanation: James A. Gosling is the Father of Java — he designed it at Sun Microsystems in the early 1990s.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Computer?
mcqs.explanation: Charles Babbage is the Father of Computer — he designed the Difference Engine and Analytical Engine.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Computer Animations?
mcqs.explanation: John Whitney is regarded as the Father of Computer Animations.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of the Computer Hard Disk?
mcqs.explanation: Reynold Johnson is the Father of the Computer Hard Disk — he led the team that built the first hard disk drive at IBM.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of jQuery?
mcqs.explanation: John Resig is the Father of jQuery — the popular JavaScript library.

mcqs.q: Who is the Founder of Artificial Intelligence?
mcqs.explanation: John McCarthy is the Founder of Artificial Intelligence — he coined the term 'AI' in 1956 at the Dartmouth Conference.

mcqs.q: Who founded Apple Computers?
mcqs.explanation: Steve Jobs is the founder of Apple Computers (with Steve Wozniak).

mcqs.q: Who created Bluetooth technology?
mcqs.explanation: Bluetooth was created by Ericsson in 1994 — originally as a wireless alternative to RS-232 cables.

mcqs.q: In which year was Bluetooth created?
mcqs.explanation: Bluetooth wireless technology was created by Ericsson in 1994.

mcqs.q: Who is credited as the founder of Email?
mcqs.explanation: Per the source material, Shiva Ayyadurai is credited as the founder of Email. (Ray Tomlinson is also credited with the 1971 first network email.)

mcqs.q: Who founded Google?
mcqs.explanation: Google was founded by Larry Page and Sergey Brin in 1998.

mcqs.q: Who is regarded as the founder of the Internet (TCP/IP)?
mcqs.explanation: Vint Cerf is regarded as the founder of the Internet — he co-developed TCP/IP.

mcqs.q: Who invented the computer keyboard?
mcqs.explanation: Christopher Latham Sholes invented the computer keyboard (the QWERTY layout).

mcqs.q: Who founded Linux?
mcqs.explanation: Linus Torvalds founded the Linux operating system in 1991.

mcqs.q: Who founded Microsoft?
mcqs.explanation: Microsoft was founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen in 1975.

mcqs.q: Who invented the mobile phone?
mcqs.explanation: Martin Cooper is credited with inventing the mobile phone — he made the first cellular phone call in 1973.

mcqs.q: Who invented the computer mouse?
mcqs.explanation: The computer mouse was invented by Douglas Engelbart (with hardware help from René Sommer).

mcqs.q: Who is the founder of PHP?
mcqs.explanation: Rasmus Lerdorf is the founder of PHP — first released in 1995.

mcqs.q: Who invented the USB?
mcqs.explanation: Ajay V. Bhatt — an Indian-American computer architect at Intel — invented the USB (Universal Serial Bus).

mcqs.q: Who is the founder of the World Wide Web (WWW)?
mcqs.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee is the founder of the World Wide Web (WWW) — invented in 1989 at CERN.

mcqs.q: Who founded Yahoo?
mcqs.explanation: Yahoo was founded by Jerry Yang and David Filo in 1994.

mcqs.q: Who founded Oracle?
options: Ed Oates, Larry Ellison & Bob Miner
mcqs.explanation: Oracle was founded by Ed Oates, Larry Ellison and Bob Miner in 1977.

mcqs.q: Who invented the ABC Computer (first electronic computer)?
mcqs.explanation: The ABC Computer (Atanasoff-Berry Computer) — the first electronic computer — was invented by John Atanasoff and Clifford Berry.

mcqs.q: Who invented the Computer BIOS?
mcqs.explanation: Gary Kildall invented the Computer BIOS — first found in CP/M and later inspiring PC BIOS.

mcqs.q: Who is credited as the inventor of the term 'Computer Bug'?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Grace Murray Hopper is credited with popularising the term 'computer bug' — after a moth was found in a relay of the Harvard Mark II in 1947.

mcqs.q: Who invented the computer scanner?
mcqs.explanation: Ray Kurzweil invented the computer scanner (and a flat-bed scanner that could read text aloud).

mcqs.q: Who invented computer speakers?
mcqs.explanation: Per the source material, Abinawan Puracchidas is credited with inventing computer speakers.

mcqs.q: Who invented the first computer?
mcqs.explanation: Charles Babbage invented the first computer — the Difference Engine in 1821.

mcqs.q: Who invented Ethernet computer networking?
options: David Boggs, Chuck Thacker & Butler Lampson
mcqs.explanation: Ethernet computer networking was invented by David Boggs, Chuck Thacker and Butler Lampson at Xerox PARC.

mcqs.q: Who invented the first database (relational model)?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Edgar Frank Codd invented the relational database model — the first database — in 1970 at IBM.

mcqs.q: Who invented the laptop computer?
mcqs.explanation: The laptop computer was invented by Adam Osborne — the Osborne 1 (1981) is widely considered the first commercially successful laptop.

mcqs.q: Who invented MS-DOS?
mcqs.explanation: MS-DOS (Microsoft Disk Operating System) was invented by Microsoft — released for IBM PCs in 1981.

mcqs.q: Who invented punch cards used in computers?
mcqs.explanation: Herman Hollerith invented punch cards used in computing — leading to the founding of IBM.

mcqs.q: Who invented the Spacewar computer game?
mcqs.explanation: Spacewar! — one of the first computer games — was invented by Steve Russell and colleagues at MIT in 1962.

mcqs.q: Who invented the Z1 Computer?
mcqs.explanation: The Z1 Computer was invented by Konrad Zuse in Germany in the late 1930s.

mcqs.q: Who invented the Computer Chip (Integrated Circuit)?
mcqs.explanation: The Computer Chip / Integrated Circuit (IC) was invented independently by Jack Kilby (Texas Instruments) and Robert Noyce (Fairchild).

mcqs.q: Who invented the first computer microprocessor?
mcqs.explanation: The first computer microprocessor — Intel 4004 — was invented by Federico Faggin, Ted Hoff and Stanley Mazor.

mcqs.q: Who is the founder of the keyboard?
mcqs.explanation: The keyboard (QWERTY layout) was invented by Christopher Latham Sholes in the 1870s — originally for typewriters.

mcqs.q: Mauchly & Eckert are jointly credited with developing which TWO famous early computers?
mcqs.explanation: John Mauchly and J. Presper Eckert jointly developed both ENIAC (1946) and UNIVAC (1951).

mcqs.q: Who developed Computer Graphics as a discipline?
mcqs.explanation: William Fetter is credited as the developer of Computer Graphics — he coined the term in 1960.

mcqs.q: Who invented Java?
mcqs.explanation: James Gosling invented the Java programming language at Sun Microsystems.

mcqs.q: What does Bluetooth replace as a wireless alternative?
mcqs.explanation: Bluetooth was originally conceived by Ericsson as a wireless alternative to RS-232 data cables.

mcqs.q: Bluetooth exchanges data using:
mcqs.explanation: Bluetooth technology exchanges data over short distances using radio transmissions.

mcqs.q: A laptop computer integrates which components into a single small casing?
mcqs.explanation: A laptop computer integrates all elements needed to run properly — battery power supply, screen and keyboard — into a small casing.

mcqs.q: Which technology was created by Ericsson in 1994 as a wireless alternative to data cables?
mcqs.explanation: Bluetooth was created by Ericsson in 1994 as a wireless alternative to RS-232 data cables.

mcqs.q: Who invented the first commercially successful laptop?
mcqs.explanation: Adam Osborne invented the first commercially successful laptop — the Osborne 1 in 1981.

mcqs.q: Which scientist is associated with the term 'computer bug'?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Grace Murray Hopper is associated with the term 'computer bug' — after a moth was found in a relay of the Harvard Mark II computer.

mcqs.q: Who is associated with inventing the first relational database?
mcqs.explanation: Dr. Edgar Frank Codd is associated with inventing the first relational database (relational model) at IBM in 1970.

mcqs.q: Computer Chip (IC) was invented by which pair?
mcqs.explanation: The Computer Chip (Integrated Circuit) was invented by Jack Kilby and Robert Noyce — independently.
